title: Crashlytics
objectives: Attach breadcrumbs, keys and user IDs

sections.markdown: Firebase Crashlytics automatically captures crashes and non-fatal errors, groups them by stack signature, and reports crash-free-users / crash-free-sessions — the industry-standard mobile stability metrics.

rows: Users who saw zero crashes in the window
Sudden spike in a crash signature
One issue can group by cause vs stack

sections.markdown: The senior habit: wire Crashlytics AND set custom keys (screen, feature flag, tenant). When something breaks in production, you can filter by those keys and find the exact context — not just 'NullPointerException in some list'.
Uploading dSYMs (iOS) or ProGuard mapping files (Android) only sometimes. Without symbolication, stack traces are useless. Automate uploads in CI on every release build.
Set an internal SLO: crash-free-users > 99.5% before shipping a release. Velocity alerts + Slack integration surface regressions within minutes of rollout.

flashcards.back: Crash-free users % (and crash-free sessions %) — the standard mobile stability KPI.
Fatal = crash. Non-fatal = handled error you still want to see (recordError).
Without them, stack traces stay obfuscated and unhelpful.

quiz.q: The industry-standard mobile stability metric is…
quiz.explain: Crash-free users % is the target on every mobile team.

quiz.q: Handled exceptions you still want reported use…
quiz.explain: Non-fatal reports give you visibility without a crash.
Without symbols, stack traces are unreadable.

challenges.text: Wire Crashlytics in Flutter and force a test crash.
Add custom keys (screen, tenant) and filter by them.
Automate dSYM/mapping uploads in CI for every release.
Set a crash-free-users SLO with velocity alerts and Slack notifications.

interviewQuestions: How do you attribute crashes to a release?
How do you triage a crash spike?
